user_query:
I need a rigorous enterprise sales review and outreach package. Read all of the following inputs under input/:
- input/pipeline.csv — my deal dataset
- input/meta.json — contains as_of_date, quota, and forecast period
- input/prospects.csv — three high-priority prospects with role, company, and a Trigger to reference verbatim
- input/case_studies.md — brief case studies you can cite for social proof

Deliverables (write every file exactly under output/ as specified):
1) Pipeline review (output/pipeline_review.json)
- Compute average_cycle_days using only Closed Won deals (days from FirstTouchDate to PlannedCloseDate), round to the nearest whole day
- Compute pipeline_coverage for the forecast period in input/meta.json: include period_start, period_end, quota, open_pipeline_period_amount (sum of Amount for open deals with PlannedCloseDate within the period), and coverage_ratio = open_pipeline_period_amount / quota (float, at least 2 decimal precision)
- Identify red flags per deal with standardized codes: no_activity_14d (LastActivityDate >14 days before as_of_date), single_threaded (ContactsCount <2), cycle_gt_3x (days since FirstTouchDate to as_of_date > 3 × average_cycle_days for open deals)
- Add forecast_categories as an object mapping each DealName to one of: commit, best_case, pipeline, omit. At minimum: any deal where all of the following are true — EconBuyerMet, MetricsConfirmed, PainDocumented, DecisionCriteriaKnown, DecisionProcessKnown, ChampionIdentified, PaperProcessKnown, CompetitionKnown — and Stage is one of ["Negotiation", "Proposal", "Verbal"] must be labeled "commit". Closed Lost deals should be "omit". Save JSON with this structure exactly:
{
  "as_of_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "average_cycle_days": <number>,
  "pipeline_coverage": {
    "period_start": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "period_end": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "quota": <number>,
    "open_pipeline_period_amount": <number>,
    "coverage_ratio": <number>
  },
  "red_flags": [
    {"deal": "<DealName>", "flags": ["no_activity_14d", "single_threaded", "cycle_gt_3x"]},
    ...
  ],
  "forecast_categories": {
    "<DealName>": "commit|best_case|pipeline|omit",
    ...
  }
}

2) Stakeholder maps (output/stakeholder_maps.json)
- For the three largest Amount open deals with PlannedCloseDate inside the forecast period, create entries with:
{
  "<DealName>": {
    "champion": "<Name or TBD>",
    "economic_buyer": "<Name or TBD>",
    "technical_evaluator": "<Name or TBD>",
    "end_users": ["<Role>", "<Role>", ...],
    "multi_threaded": <true|false>
  }
}
- Set multi_threaded true if ContactsCount >= 2, else false

3) Three personalized cold emails (under output/outreach/):
- Files and recipients:
  - output/outreach/email_apex_schultz.txt — to the VP Digital at Apex Retail
  - output/outreach/email_orion_alvarez.txt — to the COO at Orion Manufacturing
  - output/outreach/email_voyage_nair.txt — to the Head of Ops at Voyage Logistics
- Requirements for each email:
  - Start with a "Subject:" line
  - Reference the prospect’s name and company
  - Include the exact Trigger string from input/prospects.csv verbatim
  - Include one "Result:" line that cites an outcome with a percentage (e.g., "Result: 23%") drawing inspiration from input/case_studies.md
  - Contain exactly one question mark in the entire email (single CTA)
  - Do not include the phrase "I hope this email finds you well"

4) Mutual Action Plan (output/mutual_action_plan_apex_retail.md)
- One page with at least six checklist steps using lines that start with "- [ ] "
- Each step must include a Due: YYYY-MM-DD date that falls within the forecast period
- Include at least one step for Security review, one for Legal/Procurement, and one involving the Economic Buyer

Use enterprise sales best practices (MEDDIC/MEDDPICC, multi-threading, next step + date). If you make assumptions for names/roles in stakeholder maps, mark them TBD. Save all outputs to the specified paths under output/ and make sure your computations align with the provided inputs.
